name: Stytch API
what_it_does: Developer-first authentication platform offering passwordless auth (magic links, OTP, WebAuthn/passkeys), B2C user management, and B2B Organizations with SSO/SAML support — a modern, API-centric alternative to Auth0 and Okta.
use_cases: Adding passwordless login (magic links, OTP, passkeys) to web and mobile apps
B2B SaaS authentication with multi-tenant Organizations and per-org SSO
Replacing legacy username/password with modern auth flows via agent-driven user management
Migrating users from another auth provider without forcing re-registration
Embedding auth into agent workflows that create or verify user identities
not_for: Teams who need on-premises or air-gapped identity management
Applications requiring legacy Kerberos or LDAP integration without a cloud bridge
Pure machine-to-machine auth (use OAuth2 client credentials or API keys directly)
best_when: You're building a modern SaaS app or B2B product and want developer-friendly passwordless auth with strong B2B multi-tenancy support, without the complexity and cost of Okta.
avoid_when: You need deep enterprise IdP federation (AD FS, legacy on-prem) out of the box, or your organization already has Okta/Auth0 deeply embedded and switching cost is prohibitive.

auth.notes: Authentication uses project_id and secret pair sent as Basic auth credentials. Separate public tokens for frontend SDK initialization. Test vs Live environments use different project credentials. No per-key scoping — credentials carry full project access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Up to 25 Monthly Active Users free on both B2C and B2B products
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-as-you-go from $0.035/MAU after free tier; B2B pricing per Organization member; Enterprise contracts available
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for prototyping and small apps. Pricing scales per MAU, which can become significant at higher volumes. SSO/SAML features available on paid plans. No monthly minimum on self-serve.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; contact Stytch for high-throughput requirements

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors return structured JSON with error_type, error_message, error_url, and request_id. The error_url links to specific docs for each error code, which is very agent-friendly. Error types are human-readable slugs (e.g., invalid_token, unauthorized_credentials).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Magic link and OTP sends are not idempotent — each call creates a new token. Session and user operations (get, update) are naturally idempotent. No explicit idempotency key mechanism for write operations.
known_agent_gotchas: Test and Live environments require different project_id/secret pairs — easy to accidentally use test credentials in production
Magic link tokens expire quickly (default 60 minutes) — agents coordinating multi-step flows must account for expiry
B2B Organizations API is a separate product from B2C Users — endpoints and data models differ significantly
SAML/SSO configuration requires per-organization setup and IdP metadata exchange — not fully automatable via API alone
No per-key permission scoping — a leaked secret gives full project access; store carefully
Webhook signature verification requires HMAC-SHA256 validation — agents consuming webhooks must verify or risk spoofed events

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced on all endpoints. Project secret acts as a single high-privilege credential with no scope restriction — a meaningful gap for least-privilege agent access. Test/live key separation is good. Webhooks include signature headers for verification. SOC2 Type II certified. HIPAA BAA available.
